```instructions
---
applyTo: '**'
---

# ProjectFlo v1 Instructions

## Authentication & API Testing

### Getting Auth Tokens for API Testing

Many API endpoints require authentication. Use the auth token script to get valid JWT tokens:

#### 🔑 Generate Auth Token

```bash
# Navigate to project root first
cd "c:\Users\info\Documents\Code Projects\ProjectFlo"

# Generate auth token using the script
node get-auth-token.js
```

This will:
- ✅ Login with admin credentials (info@dhutchinson.co.uk)
- ✅ Display the JWT token and usage examples
- ✅ Save token to `auth-token.txt` for reuse
- ✅ Show example curl commands

#### 📋 Using Tokens with curl

**Option 1: Using saved token file**
```bash
# Load token into environment variable
export AUTH_TOKEN=$(cat auth-token.txt)

# Use with curl
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/contributors
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/equipment
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/job-roles
```

**Option 2: Direct token usage**
```bash
# Replace YOUR_TOKEN_HERE with actual token
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_TOKEN_HERE" http://localhost:3002/contributors
```

#### 🎯 Brand Context for Multi-Tenant APIs

Some endpoints require brand context headers:

```bash
# Get brand information first
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/brands

# Use brand ID in subsequent requests
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" 
     -H "x-brand-id: 1" 
     http://localhost:3002/some-endpoint
```

#### ⏰ Token Expiration & Refresh

- **Token Duration**: ~1 hour (standard JWT expiration)
- **Auto-save**: Token is saved to `auth-token.txt` for reuse
- **Refresh**: Re-run `node get-auth-token.js` when token expires

#### 🧪 Common API Testing Patterns

```bash
# 1. Get auth token and load into environment
node get-auth-token.js
export AUTH_TOKEN=$(cat auth-token.txt)

# 2. Test various endpoints
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/contributors | jq '.'
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/job-roles | jq '.'
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/equipment | jq '.'
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/coverage | jq '.'

# 3. Test specific resources
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/contributors/1 | jq '.'
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/coverage/129 | jq '.'

# 4. POST/PUT/PATCH requests
curl -X POST 
     -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" 
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" 
     -d '{"name":"Test Item"}' 
     http://localhost:3002/some-endpoint
```

#### 🚨 Troubleshooting Authentication

**Token Not Working?**
```bash
# Check if token file exists and has content
cat auth-token.txt

# Re-generate fresh token
node get-auth-token.js

# Verify backend is running
curl http://localhost:3002/

# Check token format (should be JWT: xxx.yyy.zzz)
echo $AUTH_TOKEN | head -c 50 && echo "..."
```

**Backend Not Responding?**
```bash
# Make sure servers are running
cd "c:\Users\info\Documents\Code Projects\ProjectFlo"
pnpm dev

# Or start backend only
cd packages/backend
npm run start:dev
```

#### 📊 Auth Token Management Script Locations

- **Generator**: `get-auth-token.js` (Node.js script)
- **Shell Version**: `get-auth-token.sh` (Bash script)
- **Token Storage**: `auth-token.txt` (auto-generated)
- **Frontend Token**: `packages/frontend/dev-auth-token.txt` (frontend-specific)

### Example Workflow

```bash
# Complete auth workflow example:

# 1. Navigate to project root
cd "c:\Users\info\Documents\Code Projects\ProjectFlo"

# 2. Start servers if not running
pnpm dev

# 3. Generate auth token (valid ~1 hour)
node get-auth-token.js

# 4. Load token for curl usage
export AUTH_TOKEN=$(cat auth-token.txt)

# 5. Test authenticated endpoints
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/contributors | jq '.[] | {id, contact: .contact.first_name}'
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer $AUTH_TOKEN" http://localhost:3002/job-roles | jq '.[] | {id, name, display_name}'

# 6. When token expires (after ~1 hour), repeat steps 3-4
```

This authentication system ensures secure access to all protected APIs while maintaining convenience for development and testing.

---

Provide project context and coding guidelines that AI should follow when generating code, answering questions, or reviewing changes.
```
